title: roster.query (v1) — RPC request
description:
Single-day roster lookup for the roster-adviser Unix-socket RPC. Filed 2026-05-10 with v0.45 §6.8 — third grounding-source contract feeding Phase 3's `## Constraints` section (calendar-adviser first, web-fetcher second).

Transport: Unix socket at `/var/run/ai-roster-adviser/query.sock`. Caller authenticated via SO_PEERCRED.

Returns the working status of one person on one date, sourced from the agent's local SQLite cache (synced from the W&L Log Google Sheet every 15min). Cache > 24h stale returns `status: 'unknown'` (AP-1 — no fabricated availability); chief-of-staff Phase 2 scheduler refuses to write `scheduled_start` for that person on that date when the answer is `unknown`.

ai-doer is hard-coded as 24/7 working in the agent (not in the sheet); `person: 'ai-doer'` always returns `status: 'working'` with `source: 'hardcoded'`.

Public holidays are handled via a separate (future) `holidays.<region>` feed — NOT in the W&L Log. v1 of this contract does not return public-holiday status; that becomes a field in v2 once the holidays feed lands. Annual-leave-on-public-holidays is similarly a separate per-staff pool field deferred to v2.

Privacy: `notes` on a leave/sick row (e.g. "sick — migraine") never leaves the agent; only the status enum is returned. Per the LLM-as-short-memory-expert pattern, the agent makes the privacy call deterministically.

Status: draft. Do not flip to active until roster-adviser implementation ships and its boot self-check passes once on `golden-ai-ops` (Sheets API auth + sheet-shape probe per AP-4 / AP-6).

contract_id.description: Contract identifier. Must be the literal string 'roster.query.v1'.

trace_id.description: UUIDv7 generated at the caller's entry point. Propagated to the receiver's audit row.

dedupe_key.description: SHA256 of '<caller_agent_id>:roster.query.v1:<person>:<date>'. Same caller asking the same question for the same day inside the cache TTL gets the cached answer.

source_ref.description: URL or ID of the caller context that triggered this query (e.g. an ops.db Runs row id, a task UUID, a Slack thread ts). Used in the audit row for traceability.

caller_agent_id.description: Self-asserted agent ID of the caller. Authorisation decisions key off SO_PEERCRED uid, not this value.

person.description: Whose roster status to look up. ai-doer is hard-coded as `status: 'working'` with `source: 'hardcoded'` (no sheet entry). mkkk is intentionally NOT included at v1 — MK is excluded from staff-roster semantics by the same separation that excludes them from comms-adviser (per the project_mk_exclusion memory: comms-side denial, but task / roster-side they remain a separate identity not modeled here).

date.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD). Interpreted in Europe/London by default — the W&L Log is maintained in that zone. Past dates are answered from the cache; future dates beyond the sheet's filled horizon return `status: 'unknown'` with `staleness_seconds: null` (sheet-not-filled is distinct from cache-stale).
